title: coelogyne basalis
intro: Coelogyne basalis, also known as the 'Slipper Orchid', is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Asia, particularly in India, China, and Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Coelogyne basalis include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin conditions like acne and eczema. Coelogyne basalis can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, allowing for various forms of consumption, including topical application for skin conditions.
